name: BTC Squeeze Hold Release v1
edge_mechanism: compression release with post-release hold confirmation
thesis: BTC 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing a squeeze-release idea that only enters after release bars prove they can hold above fast value. The edge comes from persistent release, not from buying the first breakout bar blindly.
invalidation_condition: Abort if squeeze-release remains too sparse, if release bars immediately mean-revert before any hold, or if the hold condition adds complexity without better post-cost continuation.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Compression resolves directionally, price remains aligned with fast value after release, and a brief second push becomes available for asymmetric harvesting.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in chop, some 4h compressions release into one more tradable leg. Requiring a hold after release should filter fake first bars without making the setup overly ceremonial.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is a clean lane for squeeze-release structure and better suited than thinner alts for determining whether post-release persistence is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If compressions mostly fake out and revert immediately, the hold confirmation will either be too sparse or too late to matter.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trade with density but follow-through is short, tighten exits before adding more setup conditions.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Selective entries, decent first-target hit rate, and occasional cleaner runner contributions when persistence is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 4h and one confirmation lane show tradable density with positive post-cost continuation after release hold.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade output, or holds that consistently fail to extend after entry.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: low; squeeze and hold are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable with broad compression thresholds

test_lanes.why_included: Best clean lane for release persistence after compression.
test_lanes.continue_if: Release-hold setups trade with credible density and positive post-cost continuation.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: The setup is too sparse or release holds do not extend.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: A modest number of clean squeeze releases with one more leg after the hold.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism generalizes across majors with comparable structure.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH shows similar release-hold behavior with comparable trade density.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Only BTC works and ETH does not participate at all.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Similar but slightly noisier release-persistence behavior than BTC.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same release-hold logic survives on a higher-volatility lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO preserves the mechanism without collapsing into chaos.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO becomes pure noise and invalidates the structure.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger release-persistence events than BTC.

long: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_55 or 4 bars
